summary: Full-stack testing of Sol & El Tech Swedish landing site. All 9 backend pytest cases pass (health, contact POST/GET, validation 422s, persistence verified via GET). Frontend UI tests pass: landing loads, sticky navbar + 5 anchor links, hero headline 'Din lokala elektriker i Stockholm', 12 services grid, about section, 4 process steps, why-us, contact form, footer. Contact form happy-path POSTs to /api/contact returning 201 with Swedish success toast 'Tack! Vi återkommer inom 24 timmar.' Empty-submit shows Swedish toast 'Fyll i namn, e-post och meddelande.' Mobile hamburger toggle opens menu, shows all 5 links, closes on link click. No functional bugs found.

minor.issue: In server.py the except block references `logger` on line 94 but `logger` is defined later on line 123. If the insert ever fails, calling logger.exception would raise NameError and mask the real exception. Low priority (happy path unaffected) but should be fixed by moving logger init above the route definitions.

action_items: Optional: move `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` (and logging.basicConfig) above the route definitions in /app/backend/server.py so the except-handler in create_contact_submission can log on insert failure without a NameError.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` referenced in create_contact_submission exception handler is defined below the route function, would NameError on failure path.

server.py: CORS allow_origins currently defaults to '*' when combined with allow_credentials=True - browsers reject credentialed requests with wildcard origin. Not blocking because app does not use credentials, but worth restricting to exact origins in production.
server.py uses `@app.on_event('shutdown')` which is deprecated in FastAPI; consider lifespan handlers. Non-blocking.
seed_data_creation: Test suite inserts several TEST_-prefixed contact submissions into db.contact_submissions. No cleanup hook was added; records are harmless but can be purged if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: https://elektriker-stockholm.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_contact_api.py - run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_contact_api.py`. All content is Swedish. Contact form uses shadcn Select (requires force=True click + short timeout before picking option). Native `required` on inputs triggers browser validation before the React toast; tests strip `required` to validate the client-side toast. POST /api/contact returns 201 with ContactSubmission (id, name, email, phone, service, message, created_at) and excludes _id. GET /api/contact returns sorted list excluding _id.
rca of the issue: No bugs requiring RCA. Only one latent code issue: logger used before definition in except block. RCA: python resolves `logger` at call time via module globals; by the time the exception path executes, the module has fully loaded and logger is defined at bottom, so it actually works at runtime since it's looked up in the module's globals() after import completes. Verified: the NameError concern is theoretical for module-level import-time calls only - in practice this works. Downgrading severity to style/ordering nit.
